name: 01_eval_scorecard
question: Which policy/checkpoint is best?
takeaway: Base model is strongest; final LoRA collapses to 3.13%, while best LoRA is step 2000 at 28.13%.
title: Baseline evaluation scorecard

name: 02_checkpoint_accuracy_ci
question: Did training improve over time?
takeaway: LoRA accuracy degrades after step 2000; final checkpoint is not the best model.
title: Checkpoint-wise evaluation accuracy

name: 03_reward_kl_timeline
question: Are reward and KL consistent with stable GRPO training?
takeaway: Reward and KL patterns support an early peak followed by late instability.
title: Reward and KL timeline

name: 04_response_health
question: Does the model still produce parseable answers?
takeaway: Empty/parse-failure indicators rise late, matching the final accuracy collapse.
title: Response health over training

name: 05_grpo_health
question: Is the group-relative learning signal healthy?
takeaway: Reward diversity and advantage spread should be monitored before another full run.
title: GRPO reward and advantage health

name: 06_reward_components
question: Which reward terms drove behavior?
takeaway: Component-level reward makes shaping-vs-task-success tradeoffs visible.
title: Reward components over training

name: 07_trace_examples_table
question: What does the model actually say?
takeaway: Qualitative traces reveal concrete failure modes behind the aggregate metrics.
title: Representative rollout examples

name: 08_failure_taxonomy
question: Which failures dominate the sampled rollouts?
takeaway: Wrong numeric answers dominate, with late response/parse failures explaining collapse.
title: Failure taxonomy from rollout traces

name: 09_training_runtime
question: How long did the observed training timeline take?
takeaway: TensorBoard wall-time gives a reproducible runtime estimate and checkpoint I/O context.
title: Training runtime and checkpoint I/O
